title: Microsorum vieillardii
intro: Microsorum vieillardii, also known as Tongkat Ali or Longjack, is a type of fern commonly found in Southeast Asia, characterized by its tall, slender stems and delicate fronds. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Microsorum vieillardii include compounds like eurycomanone and eurycomanol, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and capsules, which are often used to support overall health and well-being, particularly in the treatment of fatigue and erectile dysfunction.
taxonomy: Microsorum vieillardii is a species within the genus Microsorum of the plant family Polypodiaceae. This family of plants is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. The classification of Microsorum vieillardii places it as a type of terrestrial fern native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Island. Microsorum vieillardii is a part of the subfamily Drynarioideae, which contains several species of microsorums. The classification of Microsorum vieillardii helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other fern species.
uses: Microsorum vieillardii has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help in treating wounds and skin irritations. In some parts of Asia, it is used to treat fever and cough. The plant's extracts are also believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help in protecting the body from oxidative stress. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness in treating various health conditions.

side_effects: Microsorum vieillardii is a plant species, and as such, it is not typically ingested by humans. Therefore, there are no known side effects of consuming Microsorum vieillardii.
